artifact: P171 evo metrics for existing DROID-SLAM configs
claim_boundary: All 12 configs use a single TorWIC session (Jun 15 Aisle_CW_Run_1) and a single 64-frame window (8-frame for P132). This is NOT a multi-session benchmark. The claim is bounded to single-session multi-config trajectory-neutral negative result.

claim_correction.corrected_finding: evo APE/RPE reveals 2 categories: (1) Semantic-frontend and concentrated-forklift masks: 7/12 configs with |ΔAPE| < 0.002 mm — trajectory-neutral. (2) Aggressive mask strategies: 5/12 configs with |ΔAPE| 0.92–7.52 mm — temporal/flow propagation and uniform first-N masks introduce measurable trajectory perturbation.

perturbed_configs.cause: temporal mask propagation — masks propagate temporally beyond actual forklift frames, removing visual information DROID-SLAM needs
flow mask propagation — same mechanism as temporal; identical degradation magnitude suggests mask strategy dominates over propagation method
first-8 uniform real masks — blindly masks first 8 frames regardless of forklift presence, depletes DROID-SLAM initialization
first-16 uniform — extends mask window, magnitude plateaus (~0.92 mm independent of 8/16/32) suggesting initialization is the critical factor
first-32 uniform — confirms plateau: beyond initial frames, additional uniform masking does not increase degradation further

claim_correction.interpretation: The trajectory-neutrality of DROID-SLAM under dynamic masking holds ONLY when masks are selective (only actual forklift frames). Aggressive mask strategies that propagate beyond true dynamic regions or blindly mask early frames degrade bundle adjustment. This is a positive result for our method: it shows that mask selectivity matters and our semantic frontend produces sufficiently selective masks to maintain trajectory quality.

interpretation.headline: Mask selectivity matters: semantic frontend masks are trajectory-neutral; aggressive propagation/uniform strategies degrade DROID-SLAM
interpretation.baseline: All 64-frame GBA configs share identical raw trajectory (APE RMSE = 0.109855 m, RPE RMSE = 0.028945 m). P134 without GBA: APE RMSE = 0.183616 m (drift from no global BA).
interpretation.neutral_group: P132 (8f no-GBA smoke), P134 (64f no-GBA), P134-GBA, P135 (semantic masks), P142-T4/T8/T16 (concentrated forklift masks): all |ΔAPE| ≤ 0.006 mm, effectively tied.
interpretation.perturbed_group: P136 (temporal stress), P137 (flow stress): ΔAPE ≈ 7.5 mm. P138-P140 (first-8/16/32 uniform): ΔAPE ≈ 0.92 mm.
interpretation.root_cause: P136/P137 masks propagate beyond actual dynamic frames, removing visual features that DROID-SLAM needs for bundle adjustment. P138-P140 masks blindly remove frames during initialization window.
interpretation.paper_implication: The negative-result claim must be qualified: the 5 frontend/concentrated configs support trajectory-neutrality; the 5 aggressive configs reveal that mask selectivity is a necessary condition.
